description: Baseline-JIT type confusion in SpiderMonkey's CacheIR AddSlot attach path when the index is a huge typed-array out-of-bounds value. CVE-2025-14325 (sec-high, MFSA 2025-92, Firefox 146). canAttachAddSlotStub in js/src/jit/CacheIR.cpp previously lacked a check for typed-array OOB indices >= 2^32, so an AddSlot stub was attached for a statement that was actually an indexed store on a Uint8Array wrapped around a growable SharedArrayBuffer or resizable ArrayBuffer. The RHS value's valueOf hook calls sab.grow()/ab.resize() to change the backing buffer size, invalidating the IC stub's shape assumption; when the Baseline IC dispatches the AddSlot path against the mutated object, the IC treats the typed-array element slot as a regular NativeObject expando slot — classic JIT type confusion. The fix (commit 49150a9a555a73e21dc20181c204b6003b17c167 by André Bargull, 2025-11-11, r=iain) adds an explicit typed-array index check in canAttachAddSlotStub so the stub is not attached for typed-array indices. Tests landed separately (commit e412912d7e6ae7859f4d7f8aca3afcff053afbf6, 2026-04-20) as js/src/jit-test/tests/large-arraybuffers/bug1998050-{1,2}.js. Reporter: qriousec (AI-fuzzed).

fixes.description: Bug 1998050 - Check for typed array index in
